name: Evrima
description: Evrima is the inaugural ship of The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection and the vessel that set the template for an entirely new category of ultra-luxury yachting. Built by Astilleros Barreras in Vigo, Spain, she carries just 298 guests across 149 suites — every one with a private terrace. Evrima blurs the line between superyacht and five-star hotel at sea, offering Michelin-calibre restaurants, an open Marina platform at the stern for water sports, and a nearly 1:1 crew-to-guest ratio.

name: Ilma
description: Ilma is the second yacht in The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection and the world's first cruise ship to receive a Forbes Travel Guide Five-Star rating. Significantly larger than her sister Evrima, she was built by Chantiers de l'Atlantique in Saint-Nazaire, France, and is powered by dual-fuel LNG engines. Ilma offers 224 expansive suites including upper-category residences of over 100 square metres, six specialty restaurants, dual pools, an observation lounge, and an expansive wellness spa.

name: Luminara
description: Luminara is the newest ship in The Ritz-Carlton Yacht Collection and a sister vessel to Ilma, incorporating refinements informed directly by guest feedback from her predecessor. Built by Chantiers de l'Atlantique and powered by dual-fuel LNG engines, Luminara introduces reimagined culinary concepts, new lounge environments, enhanced pool areas, and art-forward interiors created by design studio Chapi Chapo. She continues the Collection's mission to create the world's most intimate and refined yacht experience.
